verdict.summary: Guide is mostly accurate on post-2023 ARI investment routes, but has major problems around the 2026 nationality-law status, a misleading TLDR omission of the €500k requirement for the company-incorporation route, and several internal/date/source inconsistencies.

issues.current: Потенциальная подача на гражданство после 5 лет — **03.05.2026 Президент Сегуру промульгировал Decreto AR 48/XVII**, который после публикации в DRE поднимет срок до 10 лет для третьих стран.
issues.problem: As of April 2026 this is factually/factually-temporally wrong: the new nationality law had been approved on 01.04.2026 but was still awaiting promulgation/publication. The guide also uses a future May 2026 event while the requested factual baseline is April 2026.
issues.suggestion: Write: "По состоянию на апрель 2026 действует правило 5 лет, но 01.04.2026 парламент одобрил изменения к Lei da Nacionalidade, которые ожидают промульгации/публикации и могут увеличить срок для граждан третьих стран до 10 лет. До вступления закона в силу применять старое правило автоматически нельзя считать отменённым."
issues.source: Lei da Nacionalidade; parliamentary approval of nationality-law amendment on 01.04.2026 awaiting promulgation

issues.current: Потенциальный путь к гражданству после 5 лет законного проживания при выполнении требований Lei da Nacionalidade ... **03.05.2026 Президент Сегуру промульгировал Decreto AR 48/XVII** — после публикации в DRE срок для третьих стран увеличится до 10 лет
issues.problem: Same issue as TLDR: this states promulgation on 03.05.2026, which is not correct as of April 2026 and should not be presented as an accomplished legal fact. It also risks misleading readers into thinking the 10-year rule is already inevitable/in force.
issues.suggestion: Replace with: "На апрель 2026 формально действует 5-летний срок, но после одобрения парламентом 01.04.2026 законопроект о гражданстве ожидает промульгации и публикации; если он вступит в силу в предложенной редакции, срок для большинства граждан третьих стран может стать 10 лет. Проверяйте DRE перед планированием подачи."
issues.source: Lei da Nacionalidade; parliamentary approval of nationality-law amendment on 01.04.2026 awaiting promulgation

issues.current: капитализация существующей компании € 500 000 + 5 постоянных рабочих мест, создание новой компании + 5 рабочих мест, либо просто 10 постоянных рабочих мест
issues.problem: The TLDR omits the €500,000 capital-transfer requirement for the route involving incorporation of a new Portuguese commercial company with creation of 5 permanent jobs. This makes it sound as if a new company + 5 jobs is enough without the €500k investment.
issues.suggestion: Write: "€500 000 — создание новой португальской коммерческой компании + 5 постоянных рабочих мест; либо €500 000 — увеличение капитала существующей компании + 5 постоянных рабочих мест или сохранение 10 рабочих мест, из которых минимум 5 постоянных, на 3 года; либо создание 10 рабочих мест без минимальной суммы инвестиции."
issues.source: Lei 23/2007, art. 3.º, investment activity definitions as amended by Lei 56/2023

issues.current: 10 постоянных рабочих мест — создать без минимума инвестиций
issues.problem: The law refers to creation of at least 10 jobs/postos de trabalho; the wording "постоянных" may be stricter than the statutory wording and may confuse this route with the separate company-capitalisation route, where "permanent jobs" is expressly relevant. Also, the guide omits the possible 20% low-density reduction in the number of jobs, where applicable.
issues.suggestion: Write more neutrally: "создание не менее 10 рабочих мест без минимальной суммы инвестиции; при применимых low-density условиях количественный минимум может снижаться на 20% — до 8 рабочих мест." Add caveat that documentation/practice should be confirmed with counsel/AIMA.
issues.source: Lei 23/2007, art. 3.º; low-density reduction rule in art. 3.º n.º 7

issues.problem: Internal inconsistency: the structured amount says the minimum investment is €250,000, but the note correctly says culture can be €200,000 in eligible low-density cases. If the field is meant to represent the absolute minimum, it should be €200,000; if it is the standard non-low-density minimum, the label should say so.
issues.suggestion: Either set "amountEUR": 200000 and note "absolute statutory minimum only for eligible low-density culture route", or rename the label to "Минимальная инвестиция без low-density reduction" and keep €250,000.

issues.problem: Contradicts the guide’s own text saying processing often takes "12–24+ months". A hard max of 730 days implies it does not exceed 24 months, which is misleading for AIMA/ARI backlog cases.
issues.suggestion: Use a non-capped range or raise the max, e.g. "timelineDaysMin": 365, "timelineDaysMax": 1095, with text "12–24+ months; in backlog cases longer". If the schema allows it, use null/undefined for max.
issues.source: AIMA backlog/practice; guide’s own process section

issues.problem: Future-dated relative to the April 2026 factual baseline requested for this review. It also aligns with the erroneous May 2026 nationality-law claim.
issues.suggestion: Use an actual verification date not later than the content baseline, e.g. "2026-04-22", or re-review the entire guide against the actual May 2026 legal position if publishing with a May date.

issues.problem: Future-dated relative to an April 2026 review baseline.
issues.suggestion: Change to the actual retrieval date or re-review all legal claims with the correct May 2026 state of law.

issues.problem: Future-dated relative to an April 2026 review baseline.
issues.suggestion: Change to the actual retrieval date or re-review all legal claims with the correct May 2026 state of law.

issues.problem: The citation title uses the newer identifier "480-A/2025/1", but the URL omits the "/1" identifier and may not resolve to the exact DRE act. This is risky for fee-sensitive content.
issues.suggestion: Link directly to the exact DRE page for "Portaria n.º 480-A/2025/1" and verify that the URL resolves to that act, not a generic or dead page.

issues.current: Para R&D-маршрута возможна low-density reduction до €400 000 при применимых условиях (Lei 23/2007 art. 3.º n.º 7; Portaria 208/2017).
issues.problem: The text cites Portaria 208/2017, but that source is not included in the sources array. Since low-density eligibility is a sensitive threshold issue, the source should be present.
issues.suggestion: Add a source entry for Portaria 208/2017 or the current official consolidated/source defining low-density territories for ARI reductions.

issues.current: Потенциальный путь к гражданству после 5 лет законного проживания...
issues.problem: This is incomplete for ARI applicants affected by AIMA delays. Under the current post-2024 rule, time for nationality purposes may count from the date the temporary residence permit was requested, provided the permit is eventually granted, rather than only from physical card issuance. That is a key caveat for Golden Visa backlog cases.
issues.suggestion: Add: "По действующей редакции правила подсчёта срока для гражданства учитывают период с момента подачи/запроса временного ВНЖ, если ВНЖ впоследствии выдан; но это может быть изменено новой реформой гражданства, поэтому перед подачей проверяйте актуальную Lei da Nacionalidade и DRE."
issues.source: Lei da Nacionalidade, art. 15 as amended by Organic Law 1/2024

issues.problem: The guide contains claimed 2026 updates on AIMA fees and nationality law, but the "recentlyChangedAt" metadata still points only to the 2023 Mais Habitação change. This is not a legal error, but it is misleading metadata for readers and editors.
issues.suggestion: Update "recentlyChangedAt" and "changeSummary" to reflect 2026 AIMA fee table changes and the pending 2026 nationality-law reform, or remove the 2026 claims until verified.

missing_topics: Explicit caveat that, as of April 2026, the nationality-law reform was approved by parliament but not yet in force; publication in DRE and entry-into-force/transitional rules are decisive.

Clarification of the current nationality time-counting rule for residence-permit applicants affected by AIMA delays, especially the post-2024 rule counting from the residence-title request if the title is granted.
Source entry for Portaria 208/2017 or the current official low-density-territory framework used for 20% reductions.
More precise explanation of the 10-jobs ARI route versus the separate €500k company-incorporation/capital-increase routes, including whether and how the 20% low-density reduction applies.
